name: yq (YAML/JSON/XML Command-Line Processor)

performance.notes: Go-based; fast for typical YAML/JSON processing

agent_readiness.notes: jq-like processor for YAML, JSON, XML, CSV, TOML, Properties. `yq '.field' file.yaml`. In-place editing: yq -i '.key = value' file.yaml. Multi-document YAML. Merge files: yq '. *= load("other.yaml")`. Convert between formats: yq -o=json file.yaml. Kubernetes YAML manipulation. Helm values editing. CI/CD pipeline config editing. Cross-format: can read YAML and output JSON. v4 uses jq-compatible syntax with YAML extensions. Popular in DevOps/GitOps workflows.

gotchas: v3 and v4 have different syntax (v4 is current, jq-like)
YAML comments may be lost after in-place edits
Complex filters differ slightly from jq
best_when: Kubernetes/Helm manifest editing in shell scripts
CI/CD pipeline YAML manipulation (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
Converting between YAML and JSON in scripts
In-place editing of configuration YAML files
avoid_when: Programmatic YAML in Python/Go (use language libraries)
Comment-preserving YAML editing (comments may be lost)
summary: yq is a jq-like processor for YAML, JSON, XML, CSV, and TOML. In-place YAML editing, format conversion, multi-document support. Kubernetes/Helm manifest manipulation in CI/CD. `yq -i '.replicas = 3' deployment.yaml`. v4 uses jq-compatible syntax. Essential DevOps tool for GitOps workflows and config file automation. Use jq for JSON-only, yq when YAML or cross-format conversion is needed.
